entries.label: Literal matching
entries.note: Match exact values; _ is wildcard (matches anything)

entries.label: Variable binding
entries.note: A name in a pattern creates a new binding; always matches

entries.label: String/&str matching
entries.note: Match string slices with literals; bind remaining with a name

entries.label: Boolean matching
entries.note: Match on tuples of booleans for state combinations

entries.label: Range patterns
entries.code:
match score {
    90..=100 => println!("A"),
    80..=89 => println!("B"),
    70..=79 => println!("C"),
    60..=69 => println!("D"),
    0..=59 => println!("F"),
    _ => println!("invalid"),
}
entries.note: ..= for inclusive ranges; exclusive .. not allowed in patterns

entries.label: Char ranges
entries.note: Ranges work with char values

entries.label: Or patterns
entries.note: | matches any of the alternatives

entries.label: Or in nested patterns
entries.note: Or patterns can combine different enum variants

entries.label: Struct fields
entries.note: Destructure in let bindings; field names become variables

entries.label: Rename fields
entries.code:
let Point { x: horizontal, y: vertical } = p;
println!("{horizontal}, {vertical}");
entries.note: Rename destructured fields with : syntax

entries.label: Partial destructure
entries.code:
let Point { x, .. } = p;
println!("x = {x}");
entries.note: .. ignores remaining fields

entries.label: In match
entries.note: Match specific field values and bind others

entries.label: Nested struct destructure
entries.note: Destructure nested structs in one pattern

entries.label: Basic enum matching
entries.note: Match each variant; destructure data from variants

entries.label: Option matching
entries.note: Most common enum pattern matching; Option<T>

entries.label: Result matching
entries.note: Handle success and error cases explicitly

entries.label: Nested enum
entries.note: Recursive pattern matching for tree structures

entries.label: Struct variant
entries.note: Destructure named fields in enum variants

entries.label: Tuple destructuring
entries.note: Destructure tuples by position

entries.label: Slice patterns
entries.note: Match array/slice structure; .. matches any number of elements

entries.label: Rest pattern (@)
entries.note: rest @ .. binds the remaining elements to a variable

entries.label: Exact length
entries.note: Match slices of specific lengths

entries.label: Match guards
entries.note: if condition after pattern adds extra constraint; can use bound variables

entries.label: Guard with enum
entries.note: Guard narrows which arm matches; subsequent arms still checked

entries.label: @ bindings
entries.note: @ binds the matched value to a name while also testing a pattern

entries.label: @ with enums
entries.note: Bind and constrain a field simultaneously

entries.label: Nested @ binding
entries.note: @ works inside Option/Result destructuring too

sections.heading: if let, while let, let else

entries.label: if let
entries.note: Sugar for matching one pattern; else for the non-matching case

entries.label: if let chain
entries.note: Nested if let for multiple Options; let chains available on nightly

entries.label: while let
entries.note: Loop while pattern keeps matching; great for draining collections

entries.label: let else
entries.note: Destructure or diverge (return/break/continue/panic); since Rust 1.65

entries.label: let else with Result
entries.note: Clean early return pattern for error handling

entries.label: Reference patterns
entries.note: & in pattern matches a reference; destructures one level

entries.label: ref keyword
entries.note: ref borrows instead of moving; mostly replaced by match ergonomics

entries.label: Match ergonomics
entries.note: Since Rust 2018: matching on &T auto-adjusts; no need for &Some(ref x)

entries.label: Ignoring values
entries.note: _ discards; .. ignores rest; _name suppresses unused warning

entries.label: Complex nested pattern
entries.note: Combine struct destructuring, enum matching, and guards for precise control
